data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: CDKL5 substrates -  Phosphoproteomic screening identifies physiological substrates of the CDKL5 kinase
project.projectDescription: Identification of CDKL5 substrates. Immuno-precipitated proteins were TMT labelled and analysed via LC-MS/MS.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Immuno-precipitated proteins (MAP1S, CEP131, DLG5) were separated via SDS-PAGE, digested, peptides extracted and TMT labelled. Peptide identification and quantification via LC-MS/MS, analysis via MaxQuant.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw files were searched using MaxQuant, statistical analysis was done using R.

references.referenceLine: Muñoz IM, Morgan ME, Peltier J, Weiland F, Gregorczyk M, Brown FC, Macartney T, Toth R, Trost M, Rouse J. Phosphoproteomic screening identifies physiological substrates of the CDKL5 kinase. EMBO J. 2018 37(24)

Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Velos (Thermo Fisher) coupled to an Ultimate 3000 HPLC system (Thermo Fisher)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: the top 20 most intense m/z features undergoing HCD fragmentation
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram]: Flow rate in all steps was 300 nl/min.
Comment[GradientTime]: Peptides were eluted using a gradient from 3 to 35% (v/v) B over 101 min.
Comment[Separation]: Acclaim PepMap 100 C18 column (2 μm particle size, 75 μm inner diameter, 50 cm length, 100 Å pore size, Thermo Fisher)
